tasks.title: Shared titleCase() utility (@nexus/ui)
tasks.description: Create packages/ui/src/title-case.ts exporting `titleCase(input: string): string`. Rules: split on whitespace; capitalize the first and last word and all major words; lowercase a fixed minor-word set (a, an, and, as, at, but, by, for, if, in, nor, of, on, or, per, the, to, vs, via) when not first/last. Preserve VERBATIM any token in a code-maintained acronym/proper-noun allow-list (at least: AI, HVAC, ICS, CSV, PDF, PNG, JPEG, JPG, WebP, HEIC, OCR, JSON, URL, API, OAuth, OpenAI, GPT, Anthropic, Claude, ID, UI, 3D, Nexus) — match case-insensitively but emit the allow-list's canonical casing. Preserve any word that already contains an interior uppercase letter (OpenAI, McFluff, iPad) as-is. Idempotent: titleCase(titleCase(x))===titleCase(x). Leave numbers, punctuation, and trailing glyphs like the arrow '→' intact (treat a token like 'tool' in 'Open tool →' correctly; the '→' is its own token, untouched). Handle hyphenated words by casing each segment unless it's an allow-list/inner-cap token. Export `titleCase` (and the allow-list as a mutable exported const ACRONYMS for extension) from packages/ui/src/index.ts, and re-export `titleCase` from packages/next/src/index.ts (the @nexus/next barrel re-exports @nexus/ui — add titleCase to that re-export list). Pure function, no React. Read AGENTS.md + spec/project.md first.
acceptance: titleCase capitalizes major words + first/last, lowercases minor words mid-title
Allow-list acronyms/proper nouns (AI, HVAC, CSV, PDF, OpenAI, 3D, Nexus, …) preserved with canonical casing
Inner-caps tokens preserved; function is idempotent; punctuation/numbers/glyphs intact
Exported from @nexus/ui and re-exported from @nexus/next
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — well-specified pure utility, but several edge cases (acronyms, inner-caps, idempotency) to get right

tasks.title: Relocate AI settings to hub + model dropdown
tasks.description:
Move the bring-your-own-key AI provider config UI out of the Pet Health tool and into the hub-wide Settings page, and replace the free-text model field with a dropdown of the provider's available models.
1. Create a shared AI settings component at src/app/settings/AiSettings.tsx (move the logic from src/tools/pet-health/components/AiSettings.tsx). Keep: provider select (openai|anthropic), masked API key input, save/clear, configured state showing masked key, validation. It persists via src/tools/pet-health/storage/ai-config.ts (loadAiConfig/saveAiConfig/clearAiConfig under hub:ai-provider) — that storage is fine to keep where it is (it's hub-level config).
2. MODEL DROPDOWN: add `fetchModels(provider, apiKey)` to ai-config.ts that calls the provider's models endpoint (OpenAI: GET https://api.openai.com/v1/models with Authorization: Bearer; Anthropic: GET https://api.anthropic.com/v1/models with x-api-key + anthropic-version + anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access:true), parses the model id list, filters to chat-capable models (e.g. OpenAI ids starting with 'gpt'/'o'; Anthropic 'claude-*'), and sorts newest-ish first. Export a curated FALLBACK_MODELS per provider (current known ids) used before a key is set or if the fetch fails. In AiSettings, render the model as a @nexus/ui Select populated from fetched models (after a key is present) or the fallback list, with a sensible default preselected; provide a small 'Refresh models' affordance. Never block saving on the fetch.
3. Add the 'AI Provider' section to the hub Settings page: edit src/app/settings/SettingsPanel.tsx to render a new StaggerItem > Card with an <h2> 'AI Provider' title, a short description, and <AiSettings/> — matching the existing section pattern (Appearance, Backup & restore, Calendar sync). Place it after Appearance.
4. DELETE the old src/tools/pet-health/components/AiSettings.tsx (the tool no longer hosts it). Do NOT edit src/app/tools/pet-health/settings/page.tsx here (T-022 owns removing the AI section from the tool settings page).
Keep the key excluded from export (unchanged). Read AGENTS.md + node_modules/next/dist/docs for client boundaries, and spec/epics/pet-health/features/ai-provider-settings.md + spec/epics/hub-shell/features/settings-and-theme.md.
acceptance: AI provider settings render in hub /settings as an 'AI Provider' section; old tool AiSettings component deleted
Model is chosen from a dropdown populated from the provider's /v1/models after a key is set, with curated fallback + default
Provider select + masked key + save/clear/validation preserved; key still excluded from export
fetchModels failure or missing key falls back to the curated list without blocking save
tasks.modelReason: opus — two-provider model-list fetch + relocation + dropdown wiring; correctness-sensitive and touches shared settings

tasks.title: Pet Health overview redesign + top-right pet selector
tasks.description:
Redesign the Pet Health overview into a dashboard, move the pet selector to the top-right of the tool shell, and remove duplicated navigation.
1. Create src/tools/pet-health/components/PetSelector.tsx mirroring Room Coat's src/tools/room-coat/components/UnitSwitcher.tsx: a dropdown/switcher that lists state.pets, shows the active pet, switches via setActivePet (from usePetHealth), and offers an 'Add Pet' affordance (opens the existing PetForm modal). Read UnitSwitcher first to match the pattern/styling.
2. Edit src/tools/pet-health/components/ToolLayout.tsx to pass headerActions={<PetSelector/>} to ToolShell (the shell renders headerActions at top-right via ToolSectionHeader — see how room-coat ToolLayout does it). So the pet selector shows on every Pet Health section.
3. Rework src/app/tools/pet-health/page.tsx (overview): REMOVE the 'Jump to a section' block (the duplicate View Records / AI Chat / Settings buttons). Build a dashboard for the active pet: a header/card with key facts (species, breed, age-from-DOB, sex, weight, vet/clinic — only present fields), summary stats from that pet's records (total records, # readable [extractionStatus 'done'] vs unreadable, most-recent record date), and a compact 'Recent Records' preview (a few latest records with a link to the Records page) plus a primary 'View Records' action. Keep the empty state (no pets → add first pet). Compute stats in the page from usePetHealth() state (records filtered by activePetId); do NOT modify PetHealthProvider.tsx.
4. src/tools/pet-health/components/PetsList.tsx may be simplified/kept for the empty/multi-pet management view — adjust as needed but the primary pet switching now lives in PetSelector. Static titles/labels are Title Case; the pet's name is shown as the user entered it.
Read AGENTS.md + node_modules/next/dist/docs, spec/epics/pet-health/features/registration-and-pets.md. Do NOT edit PetHealthProvider.tsx, PetForm.tsx, or the settings/records/chat pages.
acceptance: Active pet selector is pinned top-right via ToolShell headerActions (like Room Coat UnitSwitcher), present on all sections
Overview no longer shows the duplicated section-jump buttons
Overview is a dashboard: pet key facts + record stats (total, readable vs unreadable, most-recent date) + recent-records preview + View Records action
Empty state (no pets) still prompts adding the first pet; pet name shown as entered
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — UI redesign reusing an existing slot/pattern and provider state, from explicit criteria

tasks.title: Apply titleCase in shared static-copy primitives
tasks.description:
Apply the titleCase utility automatically in the static-copy design-system primitives so buttons/badges/nav/section titles render Title Case everywhere, and add an opt-in titleCase prop to user-content title components.
1. AUTO-APPLY (string children/labels only; leave non-string React nodes like icons/'→' untouched): packages/next/src/Button.tsx and packages/next/src/PrimaryButton.tsx (transform string leaves of children — walk children, titleCase string nodes, pass others through), packages/ui/src/Badge.tsx (transform string children), src/shared/ui/tool/ToolSectionHeader.tsx (title), src/shared/ui/tool/ToolNavBar.tsx (item labels), src/shared/ui/ToolCard.tsx (the tool name/title; NOT the description). Import titleCase from '@nexus/ui' (or './title-case' within packages/ui). A small helper to title-case only string leaves of ReactNode children is fine.
2. OPT-IN prop: add optional `titleCase?: boolean` (default false) to packages/ui/src/PageHeader.tsx and packages/ui/src/Card.tsx (where a title is rendered) — when true, run the title through titleCase; default false protects user content. Do NOT auto-transform these.
3. Do NOT touch Modal.tsx (T-020 owns it). Do NOT change descriptions/paragraph/placeholder text anywhere.
Verify the badges still read correctly: 'AI Required' stays 'AI Required', 'Coming soon' → 'Coming Soon', 'Available' stays. Read AGENTS.md + spec/epics/hub-shell/features/title-case-convention.md.
acceptance: Button/PrimaryButton/Badge/ToolSectionHeader/ToolNavBar/ToolCard render their static text via titleCase (string leaves only; icons/arrows untouched)
'AI Required' and 'Available' badges keep casing; 'Coming soon' becomes 'Coming Soon'
PageHeader and Card gain an optional titleCase prop (default false); not auto-transformed
Descriptions/paragraphs/placeholders unchanged; build + lint pass
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — mechanical application across known primitives with a clear rule; the ReactNode string-leaf walk is the only subtlety

tasks.title: Modal unsaved-changes guard
tasks.description:
Make the shared Modal confirm before discarding unsaved edits, and opt the edit forms in.
1. packages/ui/src/Modal.tsx: add a way for content to report unsaved changes — a `dirty?: boolean` prop (default false). When a close is attempted via backdrop click, Escape key, or the close (×) button AND dirty is true, first show an in-app confirmation using useConfirm() from the ConfirmProvider (already app-wide): title/message 'Unsaved changes will be lost.' with confirm label 'Discard' and cancel 'Keep Editing'; only call onClose if the user confirms. When not dirty, close immediately (current behavior). Programmatic close from a successful save must bypass the guard — the simplest contract: the guard only applies to the user-initiated close paths above; a parent that closes after save sets dirty=false first (or calls onClose directly). Ensure Escape and backdrop both route through the guarded close. Also add an optional `titleCase?: boolean` (default false) to the Modal title rendering for consistency with PageHeader/Card (use titleCase from @nexus/ui).
2. Opt the forms in: src/tools/pet-health/components/PetForm.tsx and src/tools/pet-health/components/RecordEditModal.tsx — compute a `dirty` boolean (current form values differ from the initial values) and pass it to their Modal; clear/avoid dirty on successful save so submit-and-close does not prompt. (PetForm renders inside a Modal opened by its parent; thread the dirty state to that Modal — adjust the parent open site if needed, but keep changes within these form files and their immediate modal usage.)
3. Use the existing useConfirm()/ConfirmProvider; no native window.confirm. Read AGENTS.md + spec/epics/hub-shell/features/modal-unsaved-guard.md. Do NOT edit Button/Badge/etc (T-019 owns those).
acceptance: Closing a dirty Modal (backdrop/Escape/×) prompts 'Unsaved changes will be lost' via useConfirm and only closes on Discard
Clean modals close with no prompt; successful save closes without prompting
PetForm and RecordEditModal report their dirty state and clear it on save
No native window.confirm; build + lint pass
tasks.modelReason: opus — shared Modal used across the whole app; async confirm-before-close flow with save-bypass is easy to get subtly wrong

tasks.title: Reusable DataTable component (@nexus/ui)
tasks.description:
Build a generic, Tailwind-native DataTable in packages/ui/src/DataTable.tsx (no external table lib, no framer-motion). API (use the CRM table's interface as the spec, simplified):
- DataTable<T>({ data: T[], columns: Column<T>[], getRowId?: (item:T)=>string, onRowClick?: (item:T)=>void, emptyMessage?: string, emptyAction?: ReactNode, sortConfig?: {key:string; order:'asc'|'desc'} | null, onSort?: (key:string)=>void, className?: string }).
- Column<T> = { key: string; header: string | ReactNode; render: (item:T)=>ReactNode; sortable?: boolean; align?: 'left'|'center'|'right'; width?: string|number }.
Render a semantic <table> with a sticky <thead>, zebra/hover rows (Tailwind + design tokens), aligned cells, and a clear empty state (emptyMessage + optional emptyAction) when data is empty. Sortable column headers are clickable buttons showing an asc/desc caret when active and call onSort(key); the parent supplies already-sorted data (table is presentational). Static string column headers are rendered through titleCase (import from '@nexus/ui'/'./title-case'); cell content (user data) is NOT title-cased. Generics over arbitrary row types via getRowId (default item.id). Keep it accessible (scope=col headers, button headers). Selection/bulk-actions are OUT for v1 (note it). Export DataTable + Column type from packages/ui/src/index.ts and re-export from packages/next/src/index.ts. Read AGENTS.md + spec/epics/hub-shell/features/data-table.md.
acceptance: DataTable<T> renders typed columns with custom render fns, sticky header, zebra/hover rows, aligned cells
Sortable headers show a caret and call onSort; parent supplies sorted data
Empty state (emptyMessage + optional emptyAction) shows when data is empty; column headers title-cased, cells not
Exported from @nexus/ui and re-exported from @nexus/next; Tailwind-only, no external table/animation deps
tasks.modelReason: opus — new generic reusable component intended for multiple tools; API + a11y + styling worth getting right once

tasks.title: Automatic durability + simplified tool settings
tasks.description:
Make Pet Health durability automatic and strip the manual posture/folder UI.
1. Auto persistent storage: call requestPersistentStorage() (src/tools/pet-health/lib/persistent-storage.ts) automatically when the tool loads / first stores data — wire it into PetHealthProvider.tsx (e.g. once in the initial load effect after the slice loads). It must degrade silently if unsupported/denied (no user-facing chore, no thrown error).
2. Simplify src/tools/pet-health/components/StorageSettings.tsx: REMOVE the 'Current posture' block and the 'Make storage persistent' button and the 'Connect document folder' control entirely. Keep ONLY the 'Export Archive' action (exportArchive from lib/archive.ts → download the zip). Keep the section description short.
3. Edit src/app/tools/pet-health/settings/page.tsx: REMOVE the AI provider section (it now lives in hub /settings — relocated by T-017); the tool settings page now shows only the durability/Export Archive section. Update the page title/description to reflect storage-only.
4. Leave src/tools/pet-health/lib/fs-folder.ts in the codebase but unused by the UI (do not delete). Static titles/labels Title Case. Read AGENTS.md + spec/epics/pet-health/features/durable-storage.md. Do NOT edit ai-config.ts or the hub SettingsPanel (T-017 owns those).
acceptance: Persistent storage is requested automatically on load (no button), degrading silently
StorageSettings shows only Export Archive — no 'Current posture', no 'Make persistent', no 'Connect folder'
Tool settings page no longer has the AI provider section (only durability/Export Archive)
fs-folder lib remains in the tree; build + lint pass
tasks.modelReason: sonnet — removal + a single auto-call wired into the provider load, from explicit criteria

The availability check (useAiAvailable/isAiReady reading hub:ai-provider) is unchanged and still correct. Do not change chat behavior otherwise. Read spec/epics/pet-health/features/pet-ai-chat.md.

tasks.title: Records list on the shared DataTable
tasks.description: Rebuild the Pet Health records list using the new shared DataTable (T-021). Edit src/tools/pet-health/components/RecordsList.tsx (and src/app/tools/pet-health/records/page.tsx as needed) to render the active pet's records via DataTable<PetRecord> with sortable columns: Title, Type (documentType), Date (documentDate), Size (fileSize), and Status (extraction-status badge per the existing mapping: pending/extracting→'Reading…', done→'Readable', empty→'No Text Found', failed→'Failed'). Add a final actions column whose cell has the per-record actions that already exist (Open/preview, Download original, Edit metadata, Re-extract, Delete-with-confirm) — reuse the existing handlers from usePetHealth() and the existing RecordEditModal/useConfirm. Default sort: most recent first by documentDate then uploadedAt (parent sorts data; wire sortConfig/onSort for the sortable columns). Use the DataTable's empty state ('No Records yet. Upload a file above to get started.'). Keep RecordUpload above the table unchanged. Static headers Title Case; cell values (user data) untouched. Read spec/epics/pet-health/features/records-vault.md. Do NOT edit RecordEditModal.tsx (just use it) or the DataTable component.
acceptance: Records render via the shared DataTable with sortable Title/Type/Date/Size/Status columns
An actions cell provides Open/Download/Edit/Re-extract/Delete (existing handlers), delete still confirms
Default order most-recent-first; empty state via the table; status badges preserved
Build + lint pass
